name: First National Bank And Trust Company Of Weatherford
description_short: Family-owned Texas bank serving since 1880, offering personal and business banking with checking, savings, loans, and digital banking services.
description_long:
First National Bank and Trust Company of Weatherford operates under the brand "First Bank Texas" and has been serving Texans for over 140 years since 1880. The institution positions itself as a community-focused, family-owned bank with deep roots in Texas communities. Their stated mission emphasizes personal relationships, describing themselves as a place where customers are "known by name" and where community support is central to their business model.

The bank offers a comprehensive suite of personal banking products including checking and savings accounts, IRAs, mortgages, auto loans, personal loans, and the Platinum Visa Card. For business customers, they provide commercial checking, business savings, commercial loans, SBA loans, lines of credit, farm and ranch loans, and commercial real estate loans. Beyond traditional banking, they offer digital banking solutions including online banking, mobile banking, payment and transfer services, treasury management, point of sale solutions, remote deposit, positive pay, and Interactive Teller Machines (ITM).

First Bank Texas differentiates itself through its emphasis on local ownership and community engagement. The website repeatedly highlights their family-owned status, Texas heritage, and involvement with local nonprofits, businesses, and schools. They position themselves as a relationship-based bank that provides personalized service and local decision-making, contrasting with larger national institutions. Their "Bank where you're known by name" tagline underscores this positioning.

The company is well-established and legitimate, operating as a traditional full-service bank with both personal and commercial lending capabilities. However, the website provides limited detail on specific rates, fees, or competitive advantages beyond relationship banking. For customers seeking a local bank with community commitment and personalized service in Texas, First Bank Texas offers a credible option, though comparison with other regional banks on specific products and pricing would be necessary for informed decision-making.
services: Online and mobile banking with payment and transfer capabilities
Treasury management and point of sale solutions
pros: Over 140 years of operating history in Texas (since 1880) demonstrates stability and longevity
Family-owned structure allows for local decision-making and community-focused lending
Comprehensive product suite covering personal and business banking needs in one institution
Modern digital banking tools including online banking, mobile app, ITM, and remote deposit
Specialized business services including SBA loans, commercial real estate, and farm/ranch lending
Community involvement documented through stated commitment to nonprofits, schools, and local support
Multiple account types (checking, savings, IRA) with both personal and business options
cons: Website lacks transparency on interest rates, APRs, and fee structures for comparison shopping
No information provided about FDIC insurance coverage limits or account protections
Limited detail on mobile app functionality, features, or user reviews to evaluate digital banking quality
No specific information about minimum balance requirements, overdraft policies, or other common banking terms
Geographic service area unclear from website content—extent of branch locations and service regions not detailed
best_for: Texas-based small business owners seeking relationship banking and commercial lending with local decision-making
Consumers prioritizing community bank service and personal relationships over large national bank infrastructure
Agricultural and rural business operators interested in farm and ranch loan products
diagnosis: First Bank Texas is best for Texas-based consumers and small business owners who value community banking relationships and personalized service over purely digital or national banking options. The primary caveat is that the website provides minimal information on pricing, rates, or specific account terms, requiring direct contact with the bank to compare competitiveness with other regional and national banking institutions.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
